tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, glycosuria, ketonuria, and bilirubinuria, with low urine specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance, suggesting impaired renal function and possible nephrotic syndrome or diabetic nephropathy. Blood glucose is normal but HbA1c is unexpectedly low, possibly due to analytical or hematological factors. Elevated chloride may indicate mild metabolic disturbance. Recommend further nephrological and diabetic evaluation, and assessment for possible liver dysfunction.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
